prompts.title: Nightly Memory Consolidation
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that runs every night at 3 AM to consolidate daily memory files into long-term storage. Finds all daily memory files from the past 24 hours, merges unique entries into your main long-term memory file, deduplicates by content match, and logs the results. Alerts you via Telegram when complete.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that runs every night at 3 AM to consolidate daily memory files into long-term storage.

OBJECTIVE: Find all daily memory files created in the past 24 hours, read their contents, merge unique entries into the main long-term memory file, deduplicate by exact content match, and log the results.

DIRECTORIES:
- Daily memories: memory/daily/*.md
- Main long-term memory: memory/longterm.md
- Log file: memory/consolidation.log

PROCESS:
1. List all files in memory/daily/ modified in the last 24 hours using exec with 'find memory/daily/ -type f -mtime -1'
2. Read each daily file using filesystem
3. Load existing longterm.md
4. For each entry in daily files, check if it already exists in longterm.md (by exact content match)
5. Append only new unique entries to longterm.md
6. Archive processed daily files to memory/archive/YYYY-MM-DD.bak
7. Write consolidation summary to memory/consolidation.log

OUTPUT: A consolidation report: timestamp, files processed, entries examined, new entries added, duplicates skipped, any errors.

TOOLS: exec, cron, filesystem

ALERT: After each run, send a brief Telegram message: 'Memory consolidation complete: X new entries merged, Y duplicates skipped.'
setup_steps: Create longterm.md with header: "# Long-term Memory" if it doesn't exist
Add to crontab: "0 3 * * *" pointing to the consolidation script
Test by running with sample daily files first
prompts.example_output:
=== Consolidation 2026-04-08 ===
Files: 2 processed
Entries: 34 examined
Added: 12 new
Skipped: 22 duplicates
Next run: 2026-04-09 03:00

prompts.title: Heartbeat Monitor
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that pings a configurable list of service URLs every 5 minutes and immediately alerts you via Telegram when any service goes down. Logs all results with timestamps. Handles timeouts, DNS failures, and SSL errors as failures.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that pings a configurable list of service URLs every 5 minutes and alerts you immediately when any service goes down.

CONFIGURATION:
- services.txt: one URL per line (e.g., https://api.yoursite.com/health)
- log file: ~/logs/heartbeat.log

PROCESS:
1. Read all URLs from services.txt
2. For each URL, run: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -L --max-time 10 [URL]
3. Log each result with timestamp to heartbeat.log: '[TIME] | [URL] | [STATUS] | [RESPONSE_MS]ms'
4. If status != 200, immediately send Telegram alert via message tool

ALERT FORMAT:
"🚨 Service Down: [URL] returned [STATUS] at [TIME]
Response time: [X]ms"

TOOLS: exec, cron, filesystem, message

The script must handle connection timeouts (status 000), DNS failures, and SSL errors as failures.
setup_steps: Create services.txt with one URL per line
Create script: ~/scripts/heartbeat.sh that loops through services.txt
Test manually: run script and verify log output
Verify Telegram bot is configured in OpenClaw message tool
prompts.example_output:
14:30:01 | https://api.example.com | 200 | 45ms | OK
14:30:01 | https://db.example.com | 200 | 23ms | OK
14:30:02 | https://backup.example.com | 000 | 0ms | TIMEOUT
[TG ALERT] 🚨 Service Down: https://backup.example.com returned 000 at 14:30:02

prompts.title: Daily Channel Digest
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that runs every morning at 8 AM, retrieves the previous 24 hours of messages from your Slack or Discord channel, summarizes key discussions and action items, and sends a digest to your Telegram.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that runs every morning at 8 AM, retrieves the previous 24 hours of messages from your configured Slack or Discord channel, summarizes the key discussions, decisions, and action items, and sends a digest to your Telegram.

PROCESS:
1. Use sessions_list to identify active messaging sessions
2. Use sessions_history to fetch messages from the target channel for the last 24 hours
3. Identify: key topics discussed, decisions made, action items assigned, unanswered questions
4. Format as a clean digest: sectioned by topic, bullet points, owner assigned where visible
5. Send digest to Telegram via message tool

TOOLS: sessions_list, sessions_history, message, cron
setup_steps: Configure your Slack/Discord channel bindings in OpenClaw
Identify the session key for the channel you want to digest
Create a script that fetches 24h of history and formats the digest
Set cron: "0 8 * * *" to run the digest script each morning
Test with --dry-run first to verify correct channel and output format
Verify Telegram bot can send to your chat ID
prompts.example_output:
📊 Channel Digest - Apr 8 2026

🗣️ Top Topics:
- Deploy pipeline: Team discussed moving to canary deployments. Decision deferred to next meeting.
- API rate limits: Engineering flagged customer X hitting 10x quota.

✅ Decisions Made:
- Ship v2.4 by Friday
- Switch to Postgres 16 for all new instances

📋 Action Items:
- Set up canary monitoring → @alice (by Thu)
- Draft rate limit policy → @bob (by Wed)

prompts.title: Disk Space Alert System
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that checks disk usage on all mounted volumes every 10 minutes and alerts you via Telegram when any volume exceeds 85% capacity. Logs all readings. Critical alerts at 90%.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that checks disk usage on all mounted volumes every 10 minutes and alerts you via Telegram when any volume exceeds 85% capacity.

PROCESS:
1. Run: df -h (excluding tmpfs/snap/virtual filesystems)
2. Parse output to extract: filesystem, total size, used space, usage percentage
3. For each volume, check if usage % > 85
4. Log all readings to ~/logs/disk_usage.log with timestamp
5. If any volume > 85%, immediately send Telegram alert

ALERT FORMAT:
"💾 Disk Alert: [VOLUME] at [USAGE]%
Total: [SIZE] | Used: [USED] | Available: [AVAIL]
Time: [TIMESTAMP]"

For volumes > 90%: escalate with '🔴 CRITICAL' prefix.

TOOLS: exec, cron, filesystem, message
setup_steps: Create script: ~/scripts/disk_check.sh that runs df -h and parses output
Test manually: run script and check log file output
Verify Telegram bot is working before enabling alert mode
prompts.example_output:
14:40:01 | /dev/disk1s1 | 67% | 912GB/1TB | OK
14:40:01 | /dev/disk1s2 | 91% | 455GB/500GB | 🔴 CRITICAL
[TG ALERT] 💾 Disk Alert: /dev/disk1s2 at 91%. Available: 45GB

prompts.title: Session Health Monitor
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that checks the health of all OpenClaw sessions every 15 minutes, identifies stale or unresponsive sessions, recovers or archives them, and sends you a Telegram digest of what was found.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that checks the health of all OpenClaw sessions every 15 minutes, identifies stale or unresponsive sessions, and either recovers or archives them.

PROCESS:
1. Use sessions_list to retrieve all active sessions with their last activity timestamps
2. For each session, determine if last activity > 30 minutes ago
3. For sessions inactive > 30 min: use sessions_send with a lightweight echo to test responsiveness
4. If no response within 10 seconds: mark as stale
5. For stale sessions: log to ~/logs/stale_sessions.log
6. Archive stale sessions using exec to remove their session files from ~/.openclaw/sessions/
7. Send a summary digest to Telegram

THRESHOLD: 30 minutes of inactivity = stale

TOOLS: sessions_list, sessions_send, exec, filesystem, message, cron
setup_steps: Verify sessions_list returns all expected sessions
Create script: ~/scripts/session_health.sh following the PROCESS above
Test manually with --dry-run first to verify correct session paths
Set up Telegram bot for alert delivery
prompts.example_output:
=== Session Health 14:45:01 ===
Active sessions:
  agent:main:session-abc123 (active 2m ago) ✓
  agent:main:session-def456 (active 8m ago) ✓
Stale sessions:
  agent:main:session-xyz789 (inactive 47m) → archived
[TG DIGEST] 2 stale sessions archived. No unresponsive sessions.

prompts.title: Automated Git Commit Watcher
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that watches a specific directory for file changes every 10 minutes and automatically commits any uncommitted changes to git with a timestamped commit message listing exactly which files changed. Never auto-commits to main.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that watches a specific directory (e.g., ~/projects/myapp) for file changes every 10 minutes, and automatically commits any uncommitted changes to git with a timestamped commit message listing exactly which files changed.

PROCESS:
1. Run: cd ~/projects/myapp && git status --porcelain to get list of changed files
2. If no changes: exit silently
3. If changes exist:
   a. git add -A
   b. git commit -m '[AUTOMATED] Updates $(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H:%M): Changed files: [file1], [file2], ...'
   c. git push origin [current-branch]
4. Log commit details to ~/logs/git_auto_commit.log
5. Send Telegram confirmation with file list

EXCLUDE PATTERNS:
- node_modules/
- .git/
- *.log
- .env
- build/dist/ directories

BRANCH HANDLING: Only commit to development or feature branches. Never auto-commit to main/master.

TOOLS: exec, cron, filesystem, message
setup_steps: Navigate to ~/projects/myapp and verify git repo is initialized
Create script: ~/scripts/git_watcher.sh following the PROCESS above
Test manually: make a change, run script, verify commit appears in git log
Verify Telegram bot is configured for commit notifications
prompts.example_output:
=== Git Watcher 14:50:01 ===
Changes detected:
  M src/index.js
  M src/utils.js
  A tests/new_test.js
Commit: [AUTOMATED] Updates 2026-04-08_14:50: Changed files: src/index.js, src/utils.js, tests/new_test.js
Branch: development
Pushed: origin/development
[TG] ✅ Auto-commit: 3 files pushed to dev

prompts.title: Nightly Backup Automation
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that runs every night at 2 AM, creates a compressed timestamped backup of your critical directories (workspace, memory, config, scripts), stores it locally, verifies with checksum, and sends you a confirmation via Telegram. Auto-deletes backups older than 30 days.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that runs every night at 2 AM, creates a compressed timestamped backup of your critical directories, stores it locally with a dated filename, and sends you a confirmation when complete.

DIRECTORIES TO BACKUP:
- ~/.openclaw/workspace/
- ~/.openclaw/memory/
- ~/.openclaw/config/
- ~/scripts/

PROCESS:
1. Create backup directory: ~/backups/
2. Create filename: backup_YYYY-MM-DD.tar.gz
3. Run: tar -czf ~/backups/backup_YYYY-MM-DD.tar.gz [directories above]
4. Verify backup with: tar -tzf filename.tar.gz | wc -l (confirm file count)
5. Calculate checksum: shasum -a 256 filename.tar.gz
6. Log backup details to ~/logs/backups.log
7. Send Telegram confirmation with backup size, file count, and checksum

RETENTION: Delete backups older than 30 days automatically.

TOOLS: exec, cron, filesystem, message
setup_steps: Create script: ~/scripts/nightly_backup.sh following the PROCESS above
Test manually: run script, verify backup file in ~/backups/
prompts.example_output:
=== Nightly Backup 02:00:15 ===
Directories: workspace, memory, config, scripts
Backup: ~/backups/backup_2026-04-08.tar.gz
Size: 234MB
Files archived: 1,847
Checksum: a3f8d2c9...
Retention: 2 old backups purged
[TG] ✅ Backup complete: backup_2026-04-08.tar.gz (234MB, 1,847 files)

prompts.title: OpenClaw Access Counter
prompts.description: Set up an always-on automation that tracks how often each of your memory files is accessed. Every read gets logged. Runs a daily analysis that identifies your most-accessed and never-accessed files, so you know what to promote to long-term memory and what to archive. Results sent to Telegram.
prompts.prompt_text:
Set up an always-on automation that tracks how often each of your memory files is accessed. Every time a memory file is read, append an entry to an access log. Run a daily analysis that identifies your most-accessed and never-accessed files, so you can decide what to promote to long-term memory and what to archive.

LOG FORMAT (access_log.jsonl):
{"timestamp": "ISO8601", "file": "memory/path/to/file.md", "action": "read", "access_count": 1}

PROCESS:
1. Create access_log.jsonl in memory/ directory
2. Create a tracking wrapper script that: reads target memory file, increments access count for that file in access_log.jsonl, returns content
3. For each read operation on memory files, use the wrapper instead of direct filesystem reads
4. Daily at midnight: run analysis on access_log.jsonl
5. Generate report: files accessed >10 times this week (hot), files accessed 0 times in 30 days (cold)
6. Send report to Telegram

ANALYSIS:
- Hot files (>10 accesses): promote to long-term memory
- Cold files (0 accesses in 30 days): consider archiving

TOOLS: exec, filesystem, cron, message
setup_steps: Update relevant memory read operations to use the tracker wrapper
Create analysis script: ~/scripts/memory_stats.sh for midnight analysis
Add to crontab: "0 0 * * *" for daily analysis
Test by manually triggering analysis and checking output
Verify Telegram bot receives the daily report
prompts.example_output:
=== Memory Access Stats - Apr 8 ===
HOT (most accessed this week):
  memory/project-a.md: 47 reads
  memory/todos.md: 31 reads
  memory/contacts.md: 22 reads

COLD (not accessed in 30 days):
  memory/old-ideas-2025.md: 0 reads
  memory/archived-notes.md: 0 reads

Recommendation: Archive cold files. Promote hot files to long-term memory.

prompts.title: Quick Memory Keyword Search
prompts.description: Execute memory_search with a user-specified query and return all matching memory entries with timestamps and relevance scores.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_search with query: "user specified topic" and return all matching memory entries with timestamps and relevance scores.
setup_steps: Identify the keyword or topic to search

prompts.title: Retrieve Specific Memory Entry
prompts.description: Fetch a complete memory entry by its unique ID, including all embedded context and metadata.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_get with memory_id: "user specified ID" and display the full content including all embedded context and metadata.
setup_steps: Locate the memory ID to retrieve
Call memory_get with the specified memory_id

prompts.title: Setup Semantic Memory Recall
prompts.description: Search for foundational memory entries and retrieve them to build a semantic context vector for improved recall.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_search with query: "core concepts preferences goals" and store the top 5 results, then execute memory_get on each to build a semantic context vector for recall.

prompts.title: Log Memory Access Event
prompts.description: Append a timestamped access log entry to track when and how memory files are being accessed.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute exec to append timestamp and access details to /home/user/memory/access_log.jsonl in format: {"timestamp": "ISO8601", "action": "read/write/search", "memory_id": "..."}
setup_steps: Identify the memory action being performed
Execute exec to append the log entry

prompts.title: Build Knowledge Index
prompts.description: Scan a knowledge directory, index each document topics, and create a searchable JSON index.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute exec to list all .md files in /home/user/knowledge/, then loop through each file executing memory_search to index topics, then create /home/user/memory/knowledge_index.json.
setup_steps: List all markdown files in the knowledge directory

prompts.title: Cross-Reference Memory Search
prompts.description: Perform two memory searches and identify overlapping concepts between topics.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_search with query: "user topic A", capture results, then execute memory_search with query: "user topic B", then compare both results and output common links to /home/user/memory/cross_ref.md.
setup_steps: Execute first memory_search for topic A
Execute second memory_search for topic B

prompts.title: Export Memory Backup
prompts.description: Create a compressed backup of all memory files and verify the search index remains functional.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute exec to create /home/user/memory/backup-YYYY-MM-DD.tar.gz containing all files from /home/user/memory/, then verify indexing integrity with memory_search.
setup_steps: Create compressed tar archive of memory directory
Verify search index integrity with wildcard search

prompts.title: Clean Expired Memory Entries
prompts.description: Identify outdated memory entries older than 90 days and archive them to free up active memory.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_search with query: "expired OR obsolete" to find old entries, then move files older than 90 days to /home/user/memory/archive/.
setup_steps: Search for expired or obsolete entries
Retrieve details of each old entry
Move files older than 90 days to archive

prompts.title: Inject Context from Memory
prompts.description: Find project-relevant historical context and inject it into a working file for the current session.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_search with query: "current project context", retrieve top result with memory_get, then append relevant context to /home/user/current_context.md for active session.

prompts.title: Update Knowledge Base Entry
prompts.description: Retrieve an existing knowledge entry, modify it, and save the updated version to memory.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute memory_get with memory_id: "user specified ID", then create updated version with new information, then write to /home/user/memory/knowledge/user-topic.md.
setup_steps: Create updated version with new information
Write updated entry to knowledge directory

prompts.title: Deep Web Research on Topic
prompts.description: Conduct comprehensive web research on any topic using multiple search queries and compile findings into a structured research document.
prompts.prompt_text: Conduct comprehensive web research on [INSERT TOPIC] using web_search. Search for recent articles, academic sources, industry reports, and expert opinions. Compile findings into a structured research document with key insights, statistics, and source links.
setup_steps: Perform multiple web searches on different angles
Compile findings into a structured document

prompts.title: Article Summarization and Key Points
prompts.description: Fetch an article from a URL, analyze it, and extract the main thesis, key points, statistics, and conclusions.
prompts.prompt_text: Fetch the article at [INSERT URL] using web_fetch, then analyze and summarize the content. Extract: 1) Main thesis/argument, 2) Key supporting points (minimum 5), 3) Important statistics or data, 4) Conclusions.
setup_steps: Provide the article URL to fetch
Model analyzes and extracts key points

prompts.title: AI Content Generation for Blog Post
prompts.description: Generate a complete SEO-optimized blog post on any topic using MiniMax M2.5, including title, introduction, body sections, and conclusion.
prompts.prompt_text: Using miniMax/M2.5, generate a complete blog post on [INSERT TOPIC]. Requirements: engaging title, introduction with hook, 5-7 body sections with subheadings, practical examples, and a compelling conclusion. Target word count: [INSERT NUMBER] words. Include SEO-optimized meta description.
setup_steps: Define topic and target word count

prompts.title: Fact-Check and Verify Claims
prompts.description: Verify claims using authoritative sources with at least 3 cross-references per claim and confidence levels.
prompts.prompt_text: Fact-check the following claims: [INSERT CLAIMS]. Use web_search to find authoritative sources, then verify each claim as TRUE, FALSE, or PARTIALLY TRUE with supporting evidence. Cross-reference with at least 3 sources per claim.

prompts.title: Multi-Source Research Synthesis
prompts.description: Research a complex question across multiple sources and synthesize findings into a comprehensive brief with conflicting viewpoints.
prompts.prompt_text: Search for information on [INSERT RESEARCH QUESTION] using web_search with multiple search queries. Fetch the top 5 most relevant articles using web_fetch. Synthesize findings into a comprehensive research brief with conflicting viewpoints, consensus points, and gaps in research.

prompts.title: Content Outline Generation
prompts.description: Create a detailed content outline including title options, target audience, sections, word counts, and source recommendations.
prompts.prompt_text: Create a detailed content outline for [INSERT TOPIC/ARTICLE TYPE]. Include: title options, target audience, main sections with subsections, key points per section, suggested word count per section, and recommended sources to cite.

prompts.title: Competitor Analysis via Web Research
prompts.description: Identify and analyze top competitors, their content strategy, positioning, and find content gaps you can exploit.
prompts.prompt_text: Research competitors in [INSERT INDUSTRY/NICHE]. Use web_search to identify top 5 competitors, then use web_fetch to analyze their content strategy, blog topics, and unique positioning. Compile into a competitive analysis report.

prompts.title: Automated Citation and Bibliography Generator
prompts.description: Research a topic, find credible sources, and format them into proper APA citations with a complete bibliography.
prompts.prompt_text: Research [INSERT TOPIC] and find at least 8 credible sources using web_search. For each source, extract: author, title, publication date, URL, and relevant quotes/key points. Format citations in APA style.

prompts.title: Memory-Enhanced Research Workflow
prompts.description: Combine prior research from memory with fresh web searches to build comprehensive updated research documents.
prompts.prompt_text: Search memory for any previous research or notes on [INSERT TOPIC] using memory_search. Then conduct fresh web research on any gaps using web_search. Synthesize old and new findings into an updated research document. Tag and save to memory for future retrieval.

prompts.title: Article Quality Audit and Improvement
prompts.description: Analyze an article for readability, SEO, factual accuracy, structure, and engagement then rewrite the weakest sections.
prompts.prompt_text: Fetch the article at [INSERT URL] using web_fetch. Analyze for: readability score, SEO elements, factual accuracy, structure flow, and engagement factors. Provide specific improvement recommendations and rewrite the top 2 weakest sections.
setup_steps: Provides recommendations and rewrites weakest sections

prompts.title: Industry Trend Analysis Report
prompts.description: Track and analyze current industry trends with impact assessments, key drivers, and future predictions.
prompts.prompt_text: Research current trends in [INSERT INDUSTRY] for [INSERT TIME PERIOD]. Use web_search to find trend reports, industry news, and expert predictions. Fetch top 5 trend articles. Create a trend analysis report with: emerging trends, impact assessment, key drivers, and future predictions.
setup_steps: Find trend reports and expert predictions

prompts.title: Automated Writing Draft from Research
prompts.description: Transform research data into a polished, publication-ready article with proper structure, flow, and formatting.
prompts.prompt_text: Using the research data in [INSERT FILENAME], generate a polished article using miniMax/M2.5. Follow this structure: compelling headline, 150-word introduction, 6 body paragraphs with transitions, and 100-word conclusion. Include 2-3 subheadings and add a call-to-action.

prompts.title: Spawn Python Coding Agent
prompts.description: Create a new autonomous Python coding agent that builds automation scripts without asking for confirmation.
prompts.prompt_text: Use sessions_spawn to create a new coding-agent session named python-automation with the coding-agent skill. Then use sessions_send to tell it to create a Python script that automates a defined task with error handling and logging.

prompts.title: Run Automated Bash Script
prompts.description: Execute a bash script that backs up recent files and runs deployment automatically without confirmation prompts.
prompts.prompt_text: Use exec to execute a bash script that finds recent Python files, backs them up, runs deployment, and logs the completion without requiring user confirmation at any step.
setup_steps: Define the bash script to run

prompts.title: Automated Code Review via GitHub
prompts.description: Retrieve PR details from GitHub and run pylint on all Python files, outputting a JSON review report.
prompts.prompt_text: Use gh to run an automated code review. Then use exec to run pylint on all Python files in the workspace, outputting a JSON review report.
setup_steps: Model runs code review and pylint

prompts.title: Schedule Daily Cron Job
prompts.description: Schedule a cron job to run an automation script daily at a specified time with automatic logging.
prompts.prompt_text: Use cron to create a daily automation task and confirm the cron is active. The cron daemon will automatically execute this task without any further intervention.

prompts.title: Automated Git Commit and Push
prompts.description: Automatically stage all changes, commit with a timestamp, and push to the current branch on GitHub.
prompts.prompt_text: Use gh to run: gh repo set-default and then git add -A, git commit with a timestamp, and git push to the current branch without prompting for confirmation.

prompts.title: Multi-Agent Parallel Code Generation
prompts.description: Launch three parallel coding agents simultaneously to develop different project components, dramatically reducing development time.
prompts.prompt_text: Use subagents to spawn 3 concurrent coding agents: Agent1 with Python skill, Agent2 with Node skill, Agent3 with Go skill. Wait for all to complete and use sessions_list to verify.
setup_steps: Define the three tasks for parallel agents
Wait for completion and verify results

prompts.title: Search Codebase Memory
prompts.description: Find all stored code patterns related to a topic and retrieve detailed implementation notes from the knowledge base.
prompts.prompt_text: Use memory_search to find all stored code patterns related to a topic. Then use memory_get to retrieve the full context of the most relevant result.
setup_steps: Define the code pattern topic to search

prompts.title: Automated Code File Operations
prompts.description: Read configuration files and automatically generate reusable code that loads and processes them.
prompts.prompt_text: Use filesystem to read a config file and write a script that loads and processes that configuration automatically.
setup_steps: Model reads config and generates processing code

prompts.title: Install Dependencies and Run Tests
prompts.description: Silently install npm dependencies, run the test suite in CI mode, and display a summary of results.
prompts.prompt_text: Use exec to install npm dependencies silently, run the test suite in CI mode outputting JSON results, and display a summary of passing/failing/skipped tests.
setup_steps: Define workspace path and test command

prompts.title: Web Fetch Documentation and Generate Code
prompts.description: Fetch API documentation from the web, parse it, and automatically generate a Python client library.
prompts.prompt_text: Use web_fetch to get API documentation and then generate a Python client library based on the documented endpoints.
setup_steps: Model fetches docs and generates client code

prompts.title: Browser-Based UI Code Inspection
prompts.description: Navigate to a local web application, extract all JavaScript dependencies from the DOM, and save them for auditing.
prompts.prompt_text: Use browser to navigate to a local web application and extract all script source URLs from the DOM, then save them to a dependencies file.

prompts.title: Send Slack Status Update
prompts.description: Send a pre-composed message to a Slack channel through the sessions_send tool without asking for confirmation.
prompts.prompt_text: Use sessions_send to send a pre-composed message to a specified Slack channel without asking for confirmation.

prompts.title: Schedule Tomorrow Reminder
prompts.description: Create a recurring daily reminder in Apple Reminders that alerts you before each scheduled time.
prompts.prompt_text: Use apple-reminders skill to create a reminder with a specified title, time, repeat frequency, and alert before time.
setup_steps: Define reminder title, time, and recurrence
Model creates the reminder with apple-reminders

prompts.title: Weekly Reminder via Cron
prompts.description: Add a cron job that triggers a weekly reminder at a specified time using system notifications.
prompts.prompt_text: Use exec to add a cron job that triggers a weekly reminder on a specified day and time using system notifications.
setup_steps: Define the weekly schedule and reminder message

prompts.title: Inbox Triage - Label Urgent
prompts.description: Spawn a dedicated session to scan your inbox, identify urgent messages based on keywords, and apply urgent labels.
prompts.prompt_text: Use sessions_spawn to create a new inbox-triage session then use sessions_send to search email for urgent messages by keyword and label them.
setup_steps: Model scans and labels urgent messages

prompts.title: Summarize Discord Channel
prompts.description: Retrieve recent messages from a channel and generate a structured summary of key decisions, action items, and questions.
prompts.prompt_text: Use sessions_list to list active sessions, then use sessions_send to fetch recent messages from a channel and provide a summary of key decisions, action items, and unanswered questions.

prompts.title: Check Active Reminders
prompts.description: Query the remindctl command-line tool to display all active reminders in a structured format for review.
prompts.prompt_text: Use exec to run: remindctl list --all --format json to display all active reminders in a structured format.

prompts.title: Broadcast Message to Team
prompts.description: Broadcast a message to multiple channels simultaneously, ensuring the team receives notifications.
prompts.prompt_text: Use sessions_send to send a message to multiple channels simultaneously, ensuring the team receives the scheduling notification.
setup_steps: Model sends to all channels at once

prompts.title: Schedule Focus Time Block
prompts.description: Create a time-blocked reminder for focused work, ensuring you receive a notification before the block begins.
prompts.prompt_text: Use apple-reminders skill to create a reminder titled "Deep Work Block" with notes and alert before the block begins.
setup_steps: Define focus block time and notes

prompts.title: Search Communication History
prompts.description: Search stored communication history for relevant project notes and return the most recent matching entries.
prompts.prompt_text: Use memory_search to query project-related communications and return the last 5 stored communications related to project planning.

prompts.title: Set Meeting Follow-Up Task
prompts.description: Create a high-priority follow-up task with a due date, ensuring you do not forget to follow up after meetings.
prompts.prompt_text: Use apple-reminders skill to create a high-priority follow-up reminder with notes and a due date.
setup_steps: Define follow-up task, person, and due date

prompts.title: Archive Old Messages
prompts.description: Retrieve old messages from memory, package them with a dated filename, and save them to your archives folder.
prompts.prompt_text: Use memory_get to retrieve messages older than a specified age, then write them to an archive file with a dated filename.
setup_steps: Model retrieves and packages old messages
Files are saved with dated filename

prompts.title: Quick Message to Channel
prompts.description: Immediately send a deployment or status notification to a specified channel without requiring confirmation.
prompts.prompt_text: Use message to send a status notification to a specified channel immediately without requiring any additional confirmation.

prompts.title: Setup Heartbeat Monitor for Service
prompts.description: Create a cron-based heartbeat that checks your API endpoint every 5 minutes and triggers an alert if the response is not 200.
prompts.prompt_text: Create a cron-based heartbeat monitor that checks the primary API endpoint every 5 minutes. Log the response code. If the response is not 200, trigger an alert via the message tool.
setup_steps: Define the API endpoint to monitor
Model creates the cron job and alert logic

prompts.title: Check All Services Status
prompts.description: Run concurrent health checks on your web server, database, and Redis cache, and compile results into a JSON status file.
prompts.prompt_text: Execute a system status check across all critical services. Run parallel health checks on web server, database, and Redis. Aggregate results and write the status report to a JSON file.
setup_steps: Define services and endpoints to check

prompts.title: Track API Usage Metrics
prompts.description: Set up hourly API usage tracking that parses application logs and alerts if error rates exceed 5%.
prompts.prompt_text: Set up API usage tracking by creating a monitoring script that runs hourly via cron. Extract API usage from logs and write metrics with timestamps. If errors exceed 5% threshold, trigger an alert via message.
setup_steps: Define log path and error threshold
Model creates hourly cron monitoring job

prompts.title: Automated Health Check Pipeline
prompts.description: Create a comprehensive 15-minute health check that validates your web application, database, Redis, and external dependencies.
prompts.prompt_text: Create an automated health check pipeline that runs every 15 minutes. Check web application, database connectivity, Redis, and external dependencies. Compile all results and send a summary to the ops channel.
setup_steps: Define health endpoint and check components

prompts.title: Configure Alert Threshold Monitor
prompts.description: Monitor CPU and memory usage every minute and send instant notifications when thresholds are exceeded.
prompts.prompt_text: Configure a monitoring threshold alert for CPU and memory usage. Create a cron job that runs every minute to check resource usage. If thresholds are exceeded, immediately send an alert to the on-call channel. Log all readings.

prompts.title: Monitor OpenClaw Session Health
prompts.description: Test all OpenClaw sessions for responsiveness, identify unhealthy ones, and archive sessions older than 24 hours.
prompts.prompt_text: Check the health of all OpenClaw sessions. Use sessions_list to retrieve all active sessions. Test responsiveness of each. Identify any sessions that do not respond within 10 seconds and log them. Archive any stale sessions older than 24 hours.
setup_steps: Log unhealthy and archive stale sessions

prompts.title: Setup Disk Space Alert System
prompts.description: Monitor all disk volumes every 10 minutes and automatically send critical alerts when any volume exceeds 85% capacity.
prompts.prompt_text: Create a disk space monitoring alert system. Check disk usage on all mounted volumes. If any volume exceeds 85% usage, send a critical alert. Schedule to run every 10 minutes via cron.

prompts.title: Monitor Error Log Patterns
prompts.description: Watch error logs for critical patterns, research potential solutions via web search, and compile a summary report with remediation suggestions.
prompts.prompt_text: Set up automated error pattern monitoring. Use exec to watch the error log. Parse for common error patterns. For each pattern found, use web_search to look up common solutions. Compile a summary report and send it to the dev team channel.
setup_steps: Model monitors patterns and researches solutions
Compiled report sent to dev team

prompts.title: Setup Uptime Verification Monitor
prompts.description: Verify your service is accessible every 5 minutes by loading the page, checking for key content, and pinging the domain.
prompts.prompt_text: Setup continuous uptime verification. Use browser to navigate to the main application URL every 5 minutes. Take a screenshot and verify the page loads correctly. Ping the domain. Log all uptime data with timestamps. If any check fails 3 consecutive times, send an uptime alert.

prompts.title: Monitor Background Cron Jobs
prompts.description: Scan all cron configurations and running processes to identify any jobs exceeding 1 hour of execution time.
prompts.prompt_text: Monitor all running cron jobs and background processes. Use exec to list all cron tabs and running processes. Identify any jobs that have been running for over 1 hour and alert the ops team with details.
setup_steps: Model scans all cron configurations and processes

prompts.title: Monitor API Response Times
prompts.description: Measure response times across your top 5 API endpoints and log them with millisecond precision, alerting when any endpoint exceeds 2 seconds.
prompts.prompt_text: Track API response times continuously. Measure response times for the top 5 API endpoints. Write all timing data to a CSV file. If any endpoint exceeds 2000ms, send a slow API alert.

prompts.title: Configure GitHub Status Monitor
prompts.description: Monitor GitHub repository status and workflow health, checking for failing workflows and API rate limit status.
prompts.prompt_text: Monitor GitHub repository status and workflow health. Use gh to check repository status and failing workflows. Compile a status report and send to the devops channel if any workflows failed.
setup_steps: Model checks workflow runs and rate limits
